summary: Completed end-to-end testing of the Ethara Ops MERN refactor via preview URL (FastAPI shim -> Node/Express on 5001). Backend: 33/33 pytest tests pass (health, signup/login/me/logout, users, dashboard stats, projects CRUD + role checks + member management, tasks CRUD + role/assignment checks, persistence verified via GET after CUD). Frontend: Login page renders, demo admin/member buttons populate fields, sign-in routes to /dashboard, sidebar + stats grid + projects quick list render, admin can open create-project modal and create a project, project detail page loads, admin can create a task via modal, member does NOT see New Project or New Task buttons (role enforced in UI), logout clears token and redirects to /login. Zero 'emergent' or 'posthog' references found in rendered DOM/HTML (only preview URL contains emergentagent.com which is platform-injected and acceptable). Theme toggle button is present and labeled correctly (switches between 'LIGHT MODE' and 'DARK MODE'). Favicon + title ('Ethara Ops · Team Task Manager') are clean.

minor.issue: When admin reassigns a task via 'assigned_to', assignee existence / project-membership is only validated on create, not on update. Invalid ObjectId in update can cast-error to 500 instead of 400/404.
CLIENT_URL=* with credentials:true — browsers reject this combo if cookies/credentials are ever used. Current frontend uses Authorization header so it works, but is brittle for production.
Pure client-side logout (no token blacklist). Acceptable for stateless JWT but worth documenting.

issues: 'Active Projects' card shows '0 TOTAL' and empty skeleton rows even when admin has projects; dashboard may have race where stats call vs projects call render asynchronously. Observed once in automation, not reproducible on reload. Recommend showing an empty-state message or ensuring consistent render.

action_items: (Optional) Validate assignee existence on PUT /api/tasks/:id when 'assigned_to' is passed by admin; wrap ObjectId casts in try/catch to return 400 instead of 500.
(Optional, production hardening) Replace CLIENT_URL='*' with an explicit origin whitelist once the Railway web domain is known, especially if cookies are adopted.
(Nit) No 'nav-dashboard' / 'nav-projects' data-testids on sidebar links — link text works, but adding them matches the testid convention.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth/JWT flow is clean: bcryptjs hash (cost 10), JWT signed with sub/email/role, middleware verifies + loads user, TokenExpiredError handled separately.
Role enforcement is consistent: requireAdmin middleware on admin-only routes AND server-side re-check inside controllers (canView, isAssignee) — good defence in depth.
server.py shim correctly proxies all HTTP verbs, strips hop-by-hop headers, and gracefully shuts down the Node child process via SIGTERM/SIGKILL group kill — solid.
seed.js is idempotent (only seeds if collections empty).
No mongodb _id leaked — all models expose toPublic() which maps to 'id'.
Frontend uses import.meta.env.VITE_BACKEND_URL || process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — correctly supports both Vite dev and the existing REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in preview .env.
Zero 'emergent' references in rendered DOM; no posthog scripts; favicon is local SVG; title is branded.
Minor: taskController.update lets admin set assigned_to to any string (no existence check, no cast protection).
seed_data_creation: Backend seed (/app/backend/scripts/seed.js) auto-creates admin + member + 'Website Relaunch' project + 4 tasks on empty DB. Pytest creates/cleans up TEST-prefixed data. One leftover 'TEST UI Project' from interrupted UI run was left in Atlas (can be deleted manually or via DELETE /api/projects/{id}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and can be re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. BASE_URL read from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env (defaults to the current preview URL). Preview proxy: FastAPI (/app/backend/server.py) on 8001 -> Node Express (/app/backend/server.js) on 5001. DO NOT edit server.py. Atlas is live. For UI, testids on task modal are data-testid='task-title' (title input, NOT a generic input[type=text]) and 'task-description'. Theme toggle is a single pill labelled 'LIGHT MODE' / 'DARK MODE' reflecting the target theme (not current).
rca of the issue: No blocking issues found. One transient UI render race on admin dashboard (Active Projects list briefly empty) — not reproducible on reload; likely parallel stats+projects fetch. No RCA needed beyond optional defensive fixes above.
